heading: Riots and civil disorders
label: 7313 Riots and civil disorders
notes: Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 90–351, title V, § 100282 Stat. 235June 19, 1968“The provisions of [enacting this section] shall apply only with respect to acts referred to in section 7313(a)(1)–(4) of title 5, United States Code, as added by , which are committed after the date of enactment of this title [].”section 1001(a) of this titlesection 1001 of this titleJune 19, 1968

Receipt of Benefits Under Laws Providing Relief for Disaster Victims, , , provided that:Pub. L. 90–448, title XI, § 1106(e)82 Stat. 567Aug. 1, 1968“No person who has been convicted of committing a felony during and in connection with a riot or civil disorder shall be permitted, for a period of one year after the date of his conviction, to receive any benefit under any law of the United States providing relief for disaster victims.”
amendments: Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. Receipt of Benefits Under Laws Providing Relief for Disaster Victims, , , provided that:Pub. L. 90–448, title XI, § 1106(e)82 Stat. 567Aug. 1, 1968“No person who has been convicted of committing a felony during and in connection with a riot or civil disorder shall be permitted, for a period of one year after the date of his conviction, to receive any benefit under any law of the United States providing relief for disaster victims.”

children.text: inciting a riot or civil disorder;
organizing, promoting, encouraging, or participating in a riot or civil disorder;
aiding or abetting any person in committing any offense specified in clause (1) or (2); or
any offense determined by the head of the employing agency to have been committed in furtherance of, or while participating in, a riot or civil disorder;

children.label: b
children.text: For the purposes of this section, “felony” means any offense for which imprisonment is authorized for a term exceeding one year.
